touches.ai_summary: Inbound request received from Finn Moore expressing interest in improving their sales-to-CS handoff process. Brief inquiry about our automation capabilities for post-sale transitions.

questions_asked.question: Do you have solutions for automating handoffs between sales and customer success teams?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, our platform specializes in streamlining sales-to-CS transitions with automated workflows and data sync.

next_steps: Schedule qualification call to discuss specific pain points

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent confirming receipt of inquiry and proposing times for qualification call. Finn responded positively and selected a meeting slot.
next_steps: Qualification call scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: Initial qualification call with Finn Moore to understand current handoff challenges at ObsidianCollective. Discussed team size, current tools, and pain points around data loss during sales-to-CS transitions.

questions_asked.question: What's your current process for handing off deals from sales to customer success?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly manual - we use Slack messages and shared Google docs. Lots of context gets lost and CS has to re-ask customers basic questions.

questions_asked.question: How many team members would need access to this solution?
questions_asked.answer: Probably around 25-30 across sales and CS teams.

questions_asked.question: Does your platform integrate with Salesforce and our existing CS tools?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native integrations with all major CRMs and CS platforms.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up qualification call to dive deeper into team structure and approval process. Finn mentioned needing to involve Miles Rodriguez (SVP of Revenue) and Sarah Yang (RevOps) for evaluation.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in evaluating this solution?
questions_asked.answer: Miles Rodriguez, our SVP of Revenue, will be the executive sponsor. Sarah Yang from RevOps will handle procurement and technical evaluation.

questions_asked.question: What kind of timeline are we looking at for implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Typical implementation is 2-4 weeks depending on complexity of integrations.

next_steps: Send case studies from fintech companies

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call focused on detailed pain points and success metrics. Finn shared specific examples of deals where poor handoffs led to customer confusion and delayed onboarding. Discussed desired outcomes and ROI expectations.

questions_asked.question: Can you walk me through a recent example where the handoff process failed?
questions_asked.answer: Last quarter we closed a $50k deal but CS didn't know about custom terms we'd agreed to. Customer had to repeat their use case three times to different people.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like for you in 6 months?
questions_asked.answer: Zero re-asks, CS team has full context before first call, and onboarding time cut in half.

questions_asked.question: How do you typically price this - per seat or per feature tier?
questions_asked.answer: We have tiered plans based on features, with per-seat pricing within each tier. Most mid-market companies like yours fit our Automations Plus tier.

objections: Need to make sure this doesn't add complexity to our reps' workflow

touches.ai_summary: First demo focused on core automation workflows and integration capabilities. Walked through sales-to-CS handoff automation, template creation, and reporting dashboards. Finn engaged positively.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the handoff templates for different deal types?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, fully customizable templates with conditional logic based on deal characteristics.

questions_asked.question: What reporting would be most valuable for you?
questions_asked.answer: Time to first CS touch after close, and tracking what information gets passed vs. what gets re-asked.

next_steps: Schedule extended demo with use case customization

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call to address technical questions and discuss implementation approach. Finn confirmed strong interest and mentioned beginning internal conversations with Miles and Sarah.

questions_asked.question: How much technical lift is required from our side for implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Minimal - mostly configuration rather than custom development. Our team handles most of the heavy lifting.

questions_asked.question: Have you had a chance to discuss with Miles and Sarah?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, initial conversations are positive. Miles wants to see it in action before fully committing.

next_steps: Schedule extended demo with custom scenarios

touches.ai_summary: Extended demo session with custom scenarios based on ObsidianCollective's specific deal types and handoff requirements. Deep dive into consulting services package, reporting capabilities, and multi-team workflows. Finn brought detailed questions and specific use cases.

questions_asked.question: Can the system handle different handoff processes for enterprise vs. mid-market deals?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely - you can configure multiple workflow templates with different automation rules based on deal size, type, or any custom criteria.

questions_asked.question: What does the consulting services package include?
questions_asked.answer: Best practice workshops, custom workflow design, change management support, and quarterly business reviews for optimization.

questions_asked.question: Are you evaluating any other solutions for this?
questions_asked.answer: We looked at building something custom internally but decided we'd rather buy than build.

questions_asked.question: How does ongoing support work after implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Dedicated CSM, 24/7 technical support, and regular check-ins included in the consulting package.

objections: Want to make sure we're not over-buying - need the right package for our size

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with demo recording, ROI analysis, and proposed package recommendation for 26 seats with automations plus consulting tier. Finn confirmed reviewing internally with exec team.

questions_asked.question: Can you send over customer references in the fintech space?
questions_asked.answer: Included three fintech case studies and offered to arrange reference calls if helpful.

next_steps: Schedule exec meeting with Miles Rodriguez

touches.ai_summary: First executive meeting with Miles Rodriguez and Finn Moore. Presented business case, ROI analysis, and strategic value of improving sales-to-CS handoffs. Miles focused on revenue impact, customer retention, and scalability as ObsidianCollective grows.

questions_asked.question: What's the expected payback period on this investment?
questions_asked.answer: Based on your deal volume and average onboarding time savings, we're projecting 6-8 month payback through improved CS efficiency and faster time-to-value for customers.

questions_asked.question: How does this scale as we continue to grow headcount?
questions_asked.answer: The platform scales seamlessly - adding users is simple, and automation becomes more valuable as team size increases.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision on this?
questions_asked.answer: We'd like to have something in place by end of Q2. Finn will drive the evaluation with Sarah on technical/procurement side.

next_steps: Follow up with Sarah Yang on technical requirements

touches.ai_summary: Second exec meeting to review formal proposal and discuss implementation timeline. Miles raised questions about contract terms and success metrics. Positive discussion about measurable outcomes and reporting.

questions_asked.question: What contract term are you proposing?
questions_asked.answer: Standard is 12-month agreement with option to expand seats. We can discuss multi-year options if that's preferable.

questions_asked.question: Are you open to a multi-year commitment for better pricing?
questions_asked.answer: Potentially - let's see the numbers and ensure we're aligned on year one success first.

next_steps: Connect with Sarah on security review

touches.ai_summary: Third exec meeting entering negotiation phase. Miles and Finn reviewed pricing proposal for 26 seats at $22,100 ACV with automations plus consulting package. Discussion around pricing flexibility, payment terms, and contract structure.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on pricing given we're a mid-market company?
questions_asked.answer: We have some flexibility depending on contract term and timing. Happy to discuss options that work for your budget cycle.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can accommodate quarterly billing with a small adjustment to pricing.

objections: Need to stay within budget - want to make sure we're getting the best value

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Miles and Finn with revised pricing scenarios including quarterly payment option and minor discount for 12-month commitment. Finn responded asking to schedule pricing call to finalize details.

Pricing call with Finn to walk through final numbers and contract terms. Aligned on 26 seats at $22,100 ACV with automations plus consulting tier, quarterly payments. Finn confirmed bringing final proposal to Miles and Sarah for approval.

questions_asked.question: Can we get confirmation that onboarding support is included in this package?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, full implementation support and onboarding workshops are included in the consulting tier.

questions_asked.question: What's the internal approval process from here?
questions_asked.answer: Sarah needs to complete security review, then Miles will give final sign-off. Should be able to move quickly.

next_steps: Coordinate with Sarah on security review
Schedule negotiation call for any final questions

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call originally scheduled to address final contract questions and close. Meeting was rescheduled by Finn due to conflicting priorities. Brief discussion confirmed continued interest and intent to finalize soon.

questions_asked.question: Any concerns we should address before we reschedule?
questions_asked.answer: No major concerns - just need to align calendars with Miles and Sarah for final review. Should be able to reconnect this week.

next_steps: Confirm security review status with Sarah
touches.reschedule_reason: prospect asked to push by a week
